Three fully laden India-linked supertankers have re-emerged in the Gulf of Oman, adding to increased observed bi-directional traffic across the northern and southern routes of the Strait of Hormuz even as conflicting narratives over the status of transits persist.

The Desh Vibhor, Desh Vaibhav and Sanmar Herald were observed in the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea on Sunday, after having been last seen signaling their attempt to cross the Strait of Hormuz late Friday, ship-tracking data compiled by Bloomberg News show.

The supertankers, each signaling Indian ownership or India-bound cargo, carry between them nearly 6 million barrels of Iraqi and Kuwaiti oil. It couldn’t be immediately determined the routes that these tankers took, but their attempts to sail toward Qeshm island suggest they may have taken a Tehran-approved one

Iran announced on June 20 that it had closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing continued Israeli str!kes in Lebanon, while US Vice President JD Vance and military officials d!sputed the claim, saying commercial shipping remained operational. The confl!cting accounts emerged amid ongoing US-Iran talks in Switzerland and have heightened concerns about global energy supplies, maritime security and regional stability. Energy markets and shipping firms are closely monitoring developments as diplomats seek to prevent further escalation.

👉🏼 Why Hormuz Matters

The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most important energy shipping routes, carrying a substantial share of global oil and liquefied natural gas trade. Iranian officials warned that further measures could follow if attⱥcks in Lebanon continue. Meanwhile, US officials maintained that vessels were still transiting the route, underscoring the differing narratives surrounding the situation.

👉🏼 Latest Developments And Wider Context

The announcement followed renewed Israeli str!kes in Lebanon, which Iran described as a violation of efforts aimed at reducing regional tensions. The development comes as senior American and Iranian officials continue negotiations in Switzerland on broader security and diplomatic issues. Global energy markets, insurers and shipping companies are closely watching the situation amid concerns over potential disruptions and increased costs.

👉🏼 The Logical Indian's Perspective

Periods of heightened geopolitical tension highlight the importance of dialogue, restraint and diplomacy. Disputes involving critical international trade routes can affect people far beyond the confl!ct zone through economic uncertainty and rising costs. Sustainable peace requires cooperation, accountability and respect for international commitments. Do you think diplomatic engagement can help reduce tensions and prevent a wider regional cr!sis?

What’s going on with US-Iran talks and the Strait of Hormuz?
Washington and Tehran are sending conflicting messages ahead of follow-up discussions in Switzerland
Published 21 Jun, 2026
https://t.co/ESV3pkHQSg
The situation in the Strait of Hormuz remains tense following the signing of a memorandum of understanding between the US and Iran, as the countries’ delegations descend on Switzerland for follow-up discussions.
Commercial shipping through the key waterway was disrupted after the US-Israeli attack against Iran in February, and reopening it was one of the key points of the memorandum signed by US President Donald Trump on Wednesday. Washington promised to lift its own blockade of Iranian ports, while Tehran will gradually “make arrangements using its best efforts for the safe passage of commercial vessels.”
Trump touted the preliminary deal as an “immediate reopening,” announcing that “the oil is flowing” after the first tankers sailed through – before Iran claimed that it shut down the waterway again.
What does Iran say?
Iran accused the US of failing to fulfill its commitments and force Israel to stop its “crimes” in Lebanon. The Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters military command announced on Saturday that “the Strait of Hormuz is closed,” while the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned vessels against approaching the waterway, saying their safety would be “jeopardized.”
What does the US say?
US Vice President J.D. Vance denied that traffic through the strait was interrupted, insisting that “we actually got 16 million barrels of oil out of the Strait of Hormuz yesterday.” In an interview with Fox on Saturday, Vance called it “a record going back to even before the conflict started.”
US Central Command stated that “commercial ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz increased June 20,” while US forces continue to operate in the area. According to CENTCOM data, safe passage through the waterway “remained intact,” and at least 55 merchant vessels passed through as of Saturday.
What is actually happening?
Dozens of vessels, including three Saudi oil supertankers and five sanctioned Iranian ships, have moved through the waterway in recent days, according to data analytics company Kpler, which noted that most were “following established Iranian route patterns.”...link to read full article

The toll question reveals the underlying leverage structure. If the US imposes one, it claims formal control over an international waterway — legally complex, especially with China, India, and Japan watching. If Iran imposes one unilaterally, it's a sanctions violation and a casus belli. Neither happens: the toll was always rhetorical positioning. What matters commercially is the insurance premium, which is already a de facto toll that markets pay regardless of official announcements. Brent at $80 with US crude inventories at 1985 lows is not a fearful price; it's a market that hasn't fully priced the physical picture yet.The toll question reveals the underlying leverage structure. @mikemcglone11 The key issue is that you’re extrapolating a regional balance  into a global crude price call.
The physical market still looks tight: US crude inventories remain below 5-year norms, Cushing sits roughly 37% below its 5-year average, and the Brent curve remains backwardated.

"The good news: The Strait of Hormuz is open again, after Iran and the United States signed a memorandum of understanding this week.

The bad news: It may be too late.

Oil hasn’t been coming out of the Middle East for nearly four months. All told, the world lost 1.15 billion barrels of oil supply during the war, according to analytics firm Kpler.
That has left the oil market in a precarious state, and it’s rapidly approaching a breaking point. 

The International Energy Administration’s strategic petroleum reserves are at their lowest levels since 1990. The American emergency reserve is at a 43-year low. And commercial inventories have hit operational stress levels.

“You want to see bedlam?” President Donald Trump said at the G7 in Versailles Wednesday. “We run out of reserves in about four weeks.”

Trump is right. But reopening the strait this week may not get oil out of the Persian Gulf fast enough to prevent crude inventories from effectively running to empty.

Oil prices may have to go higher again."

CNN

Week-Ahead Brief | June 22-26 2026 | SPX 7500.58 close (Thursday OPEX)

The cheapest options of the year going into the biggest macro week. Five days mapped. Daily levels and scenarios.

Goldman warned Friday that this week looks calm but isn't. Here is why.

Three signals are all pricing the same thing. Monday options trade at 8.91 percent vol, the cheapest pricing since Christmas holiday week. COR1M sits at 8.02. Below 8 is extreme. When correlation snaps back, the move is usually violent. June OPEX cleared Thursday with more call positioning than puts, leaving dealer hedges to unwind Monday and Tuesday. Three asymmetric signals stacked into a week with Core PCE Thursday.

The week builds straight into PCE. Monday Fed Waller speech 9 AM kicks off post-FOMC commentary. Tuesday PMI Flash gives the first economic activity read. Wednesday brings Stress Test results plus Micron earnings after-hours. Thursday Core PCE is the headline event. Friday closes Q2 with pension rebalancing flow last 30 minutes.

Iran closed Hormuz again Saturday as talks open in Switzerland Sunday with Lebanon attacks still live. Watch Brent at the Monday open for the inflation read.

Thursday closed at 7500.58 and took back the pivot. 7500 anchors the week. 7440 to 7480 EMA confluence holds the floor. Above 7600 the post-OPEX residual call wall caps the upside. Below 7400 is the structural risk zone if PCE comes in hot.

THE WEEK AT A GLANCE

Mon 6/22 (CAUTION flag): Fed Waller speech 9 AM. Post-OPEX dealer unwind window starts. Light data day. Bill auctions only.

Tue 6/23 (SQUEEZE flag): ADP weekly 8:15 AM. S&P Global PMI Flash 9:45 AM (first activity read). Richmond Fed 10 AM. 2-Year Note Auction 1 PM. CCL pre-market and FDX after-hours earnings.

Wed 6/24 (CALM flag): Building Permits and New Home Sales 10 AM. EIA Crude 10:30 AM. 5-Year Note Auction 1 PM. Fed Bank Stress Test Results 4 PM. Micron earnings after-hours (11.98 percent implied move). TCOM after-hours.

Thu 6/25 (SQUEEZE flag): Core PCE Price Index 8:30 AM. Durable Goods 8:30 AM. GDP Q1 Final 8:30 AM. Jobless Claims 8:30 AM. Fed Williams 3:40 PM. Fed Goolsbee 6:30 PM. The headline event of the week.

Fri 6/26 (CALM flag): Goods Trade Balance 8:30 AM. Michigan Sentiment Final 10 AM. Baker Hughes Rig Count 1 PM. Last full trading day of Q2. Quarter End rebalancing flow into the close.

THE VOL PICTURE

Vol is priced for a quiet week into PCE Thursday. That pricing is the asymmetric part of the setup.

Monday at-the-money options trade at 8.91 percent IV. Goldman flagged that level approaches the 7 percent lower bound seen during the Christmas holiday week. That is holiday-week pricing for a week that has Fed Waller speaking Monday morning, PMI Flash Tuesday, Stress Test results Wednesday, Core PCE Thursday, and quarter-end Friday. The options market is paying almost nothing for protection on Monday and Tuesday.

The straddles tell the same story. Monday options price an 85 point expected move from the close. Friday options price 110 points. The 25 point gap is the entire week of risk premium. Most of it lives in Thursday's PCE print and Friday's month-end flow.

Goldman also flagged the structural imbalance from Thursday's OPEX. The June expiry cycle had more call delta notional than puts. When those positions roll off, dealer hedges associated with them unwind, and that can contribute to elevated volatility. The institutional research desks are openly warning about Monday and Tuesday being trap days.

VVIX sits at 88.43, right at its historical mean of 85. After running to 94.53 Wednesday on the hawkish dot plot, vol-of-vol normalized into Thursday's OPEX rally. Going into a PCE week with the dot plot now showing rate hikes as a real possibility, VVIX at the historical baseline is not paying up for protection. That mirrors what the index options market is doing.

The vol curve says the market thinks Monday and Tuesday are sleepy then vol builds into PCE Thursday and quarter-end Friday. If post-OPEX dealer unwind hits as Goldman called out, that assumption breaks fast and Monday or Tuesday becomes the surprise vol expansion.

WHAT HAPPENED THURSDAY OPEX

SPX closed 7500.58, up 80.48 points. Opened 7487.36. Session low 7468.32. Session high 7511.07. Reclaimed the 7500 pivot that broke Wednesday post-FOMC. Closed RIGHT AT the level. Range only 42.75 points which is tight for an OPEX day. Vol absorbed cleanly.

Dow +0.14 percent. Russell 2000 +2.12 percent (smallcaps led). Nasdaq +1.91 percent on Intel-Apple chip news. Brent oil dropped on Iran deal optimism then bounced back to $80 Friday on Hormuz uncertainty.

AAII Sentiment for week ending 6/17 showed Bullish 36.6 percent, Bearish 39.4 percent. More bearish than bullish, more bearish than the historical average of 31 percent. That is a contrarian-bullish positioning setup, especially with smallcaps showing the strongest sentiment in the special question at 27.4 percent.

The Sep 15 Fed meeting now prices 50 percent No Change and 38 percent 25 bps Hike. Before Wednesday the hike probability was near zero. The hawkish dot plot repriced the curve. PCE Thursday is the next test.

THE WEEK'S BIG STORY

Four threads pull on this week.

1. Post-OPEX dealer unwind Monday and Tuesday. Goldman flagged June OPEX had more call delta notional than puts. When call hedges unwind without put hedges balancing them, the unwind tilts the tape. Vol can expand from compressed levels fast.

2. PCE Thursday is the gravity center. Core PCE is the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. After a hawkish dot plot now showing 38 percent hike probability at September, any upside surprise puts hike pricing above 50 percent. Below 7400 is the structural risk zone if PCE comes in hot.

3. Iran 60-day clock and Hormuz. Iran declared Hormuz closed Saturday, calling it the IRGC's first operational step. US-Iran talks open Sunday in Switzerland. Markets have so far dismissed the closure as rhetoric since 55 ships transited Hormuz Saturday despite the announcement. But Brent at $80 and Israel still attacking Lebanon mean energy can spike Monday open if Sunday talks fail. The peace deal is fragile.

4. Quarter-end Friday. Pension rebalancing flow possible last 30 minutes of the session. Q2 ends Friday June 26 since June 30 is a Tuesday. Watch closing tape.

🟡 7500   anchor pivot
🔴 7480   drops fast if it cracks
🟢 7475   strong support · Tuesday pivot still in play
🟢 7460   support
⚡ 7450   drops fast if it cracks

🟢 7400   strong support
🟢 7385   strong support
🟢 7335   strong support · deep catch
🟢 7325   major support cluster

IF THEN (Wednesday 6/24):

🔼 IF SPX holds 7475 and pushes above 7500
   First test is 7525 next, then 7530 magnet, then 7560 strong resistance
   Above 7584 straddle top opens 7600 mega cluster destination. Bullish day on calm positioning ahead of MU after-hours and Thursday's PCE.

⏸ IF SPX trades between 7475 and 7530
   Range-bound day inside the magnet zone while traders wait for MU earnings and Stress Test results
   Premium decays as expected on the CALM flag day. Stay flat or trade small until either end breaks.

🔽 IF SPX loses 7475 and 7460
   THEN 7450 cracks lower, 7430 support area next
   The bearish scenario where Tuesday's pivot fails ahead of MU and traders position for a defensive close.

🔽 IF SPX loses 7430 and 7417 straddle bottom
   THEN 7400 strong support catches first
   Below 7400 the deep catch zone at 7385 and 7335 holds. 7325 is the major destination if real damage happens before PCE.

The story Wednesday: the map firms up. Resistance builds at 7525 and 7560. Support stacks below 7475. The map turns bullish if MU earnings beat after-hours. Stress Test results at 4 PM matter for bank sector reaction Thursday open.

THURSDAY JUNE 25 PREVIEW - PCE DAY

SQUEEZE flag. The headline event of the week. Core PCE 8:30 AM.

Key catalysts: Core PCE Price Index MoM and YoY 8:30 AM. Durable Goods 8:30 AM. GDP Growth Rate Q1 Final 8:30 AM. Personal Income and Spending 8:30 AM. Initial Jobless Claims 8:30 AM. Continuing Claims 8:30 AM. Jobless Claims 4-Week Average 8:30 AM. 7-Year Note Auction 1 PM. Fed Williams 3:40 PM. Fed Goolsbee 6:30 PM.

This is the busiest 8:30 AM data drop of the year so far. PCE is the headline but the package matters. Strong durable goods + hot PCE = the hawkish double-tap. Weak durable goods + cool PCE = relief rally.

Straddle: +/- 98.45 ref 7500.58 (7402 to 7599)
Key levels for Thursday:
The PCE day has a very different structure than Mon/Tue. The book shows:
7600 strong fuel up with put support
7565 light support
7475 light support with put support (Tuesday's cluster faded)
7470 strong support
7450 STRONG support cluster - new magnet zone
7430 put support
7400 magnet with put support
7350 light fuel up
7320 strong support · deep catch
7310 drops fast if it cracks
7275 strong support

The 7320 and 7310 levels look like a paired position - support catches at 7320 with acceleration just below at 7310. Likely an insurance trade ahead of PCE. Watch how it develops.

If PCE comes in line, vol compresses and we drift back toward the 7500 anchor.
If PCE comes hot (above expectations), 7450 is first catch and 7400 magnet pulls.
If PCE comes cool, 7565 and 7600 zone opens for a relief rally.

FRIDAY JUNE 26 PREVIEW - QUARTER END

CALM flag. Last full trading day of Q2. Quarter End rebalancing flow into the close.

Key catalysts: Goods Trade Balance 8:30 AM. Wholesale and Retail Inventories. Michigan Sentiment Final and 5-Year Inflation Expectations 10 AM. Baker Hughes Rig Count 1 PM.

Straddle: +/- 110.15 ref 7500.58 (7390 to 7611) - widest of the week

Key levels for Friday:
7550 sellers stacked heavy (-552 / -624) = fuel up zone
7545 sellers stacked
7535 light fuel up with put support
7500 anchor with put support rebuilt
7485 strong support
7460 drops fast if it cracks
7430 strong support
7425 strong support, well defended
7400 strong support
7375 drops fast if it cracks
7340 strong support · deep catch

Quarter-end positioning typically tilts directional based on equity performance Q2. If Q2 ends positive (which is likely given Thursday's recovery), pension rebalancing buys bonds and sells equities the last 30 minutes. Watch closing tape closely. Could be the most directional last 30 minutes of the month.

THE WEEK'S RISKS

1. Goldman's post-OPEX dealer unwind warning Monday and Tuesday. Vol priced for calm. The map shows 7660 and 7650 call walls from OPEX residual on Monday that vanish Tuesday. The transition between days is where vol can fire.

2. PCE Thursday is the gravity center. Hawkish dot plot showed 38 percent hike probability at September meeting. Hot PCE puts hike pricing above 50 percent. Cool PCE relieves the entire curve.

3. Iran Hormuz closure and Lebanon. Talks open Switzerland Sunday. If they fail or stall, Brent pops Monday open and feeds the inflation story into PCE Thursday. 55 ships still transited Hormuz Saturday so the immediate impact is rhetoric not reality. But the situation is fragile.

4. Quarter-end Friday pension rebalancing. If Q2 ends positive, expect equity selling and bond buying last 30 minutes. Could be a setup for a Monday June 29 reversal if positioning unwinds.

RULES

1. Vol is priced at holiday-week levels. The setup is asymmetric. Trade smaller until the post-OPEX unwind window clears.

2. 7500 is the anchor for the entire week. Hold it Monday and bulls have a path. Lose it and the structure changes.

3. 7440 to 7480 is the moving average confluence zone. 20D EMA 7441.01 + 20D SMA 7481.88. The strongest technical support cluster of the week sits here.

4. Goldman warned about post-OPEX dealer unwind hitting Monday and Tuesday. Vol can expand fast from compressed levels. Treat all levels as breakable Monday and Tuesday.

5. The 7345 acceleration zone on Monday's put map is the worst-case break. Massive sellers stacked. If 7350 fails, the move accelerates.

6. PCE Thursday is the headline event. Position smaller into the print. The 8:30 AM data drop includes PCE + Durable Goods + GDP + Jobless Claims all at once. Most catalyst-heavy moment of the year so far.

7. The 7320 and 7310 paired levels on Thursday's book look like an insurance trade. Watch how they develop into PCE.

8. Friday is quarter-end. Last 30 minutes can be one-directional based on Q2 performance. Stay flat or hedge into the close.

9. Iran headlines from Sunday's Switzerland talks set the Monday open tone. If Brent pops, the inflation story for PCE Thursday gets louder.

10. Above 7500 we stay bullish into PCE. Below 7400 we stay bearish into PCE. The middle is the chop zone where most of the week lives.

Not financial advice. Verify all levels before trading.

I grew up in a neighborhood where dying felt easier than surviving the next 24 hours in peace. This is Nigeria, the real one. Many communities like mine have never even heard of X. As painful as it sounds, it’s the truth. The government knows me. They know my village. They know our names, our faces, our soil. Yet they have nothing to show for it. no gratitude, no development, no mercy. They extract our oil by the millions of barrels, sell it to the world, and leave us in total darkness. I was almost a grown man before I first experienced steady electricity… which was the day I left the village.
And what made the pain sharper was finally learning the price of a single oil tanker. The figures hit me like ice in my veins. Our wealth, stolen and paraded in plain sight, while we drank from polluted streams and lived by candlelight

🇦🇪 MBDA and Fly-R signed a contract to conduct the rapid development of Diamond Shaped loitering munition in the UAE, during Eurosatory 2026. @MBDAGroup 

This contract follows agreements signed at Dubai Airshow 2025 between MBDA and Tawazun Council to establish strategic projects supporting localization of defence technology in the UAE. The new contract involves developing, qualifying and preparing the mass-production of the loitering munition in the UAE through the newly-established MBDA Landed Company.

The Diamond Shaped loitering munition is tube-launched, light and compact, rapidly deployable, easy to operate, and can be remotely-controlled for precision strikes on critical targets. It provides up to 60 minutes of endurance and up to 50 km of range for day and night reconnaissance and strike capability and can be either man-portable or fitted on various land/naval/air platforms.

The loitering munition will be able to engage fixed or mobile targets on the ground or at sea - such as armored vehicles, infrastructure, fast inshore attack crafts, unmanned surface vehicles -  at distances up to 50 km.

#MBDA #FlyR #UAE #MBDALandedCompany #DiamondShaped #Eurosatory2026

Cross-Commodity Transmission Mechanisms:

Based on the above, the commodities above have three key transmission channels which can be  can be summized as:

(i.)The monetary channel, where the feds funds rate and other key central bank monetary policy by extension, USD value v concerned currency and liquidity concerns affect commodity pricing; 

(ii.) The real economy channel: this affects silver and oil prices to a larger extent, as both commodities are dependent on industrial production, manufacturing cycles, and energy consumption, in addition to the monetary variables), and; 

iii)The geopolitical channels: OPEC Supply Dynamics (whether OPEC is pursuing a profit maximization or a market share preservation strategy) sanctions and inventory shocks. 

Based on the above we present our forecasts for three time periods (182-days, 364-days, 546-days.)

Forecast Scenarios:

Gold: 6M $4,350 | 12M $4,900 | 18M $5,500 

Silver: 6M $70.15 | 12M $77.58 | 18M $87.50 

Crude Oil [BRENT-WTI AVG]: 6M $77.50–80.65 | 12M $72.50–79.40 | 18M $80–85
